description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, dark metal head. The handle is made from a light-colored wood, smoothly finished and ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip, with a gentle curve that aids in swinging. The axe head is broad and rectangular, with a pronounced poll and a single sharp cutting edge, showing a weathered, mottled brown patina typical of forged steel. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with the wood passing through the eye of the blade and slightly protruding at the top. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, giving the tool a utilitarian, traditional appearance. This type of axe is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds for chopping wood or general utility tasks.
description: A traditional hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal head, designed for chopping and utility work.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, rectangular metal axe head.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, highlighting its rounded cross-section and the alignment of the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the handle's curve and the other face of the metal head.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the eye of the axe head and how the handle is mounted through it.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, smooth finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad rectangular blade with a flat poll and a slightly beveled cutting edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle passing through the eye of the blade.
